results.title: Spindrift Sparkling Water: The Best Bubbly of Summer | Epicurious
results.author: David Tamarkin
results.text:
Spindrift Sparkling Water: The Best Bubbly of Summer | Epicurious

Skip to main content

Save this story

Save this story

I fell in love with Spindrift last summer, though it may be more accurate to say that I fell in lust. Spindrift, if you don't know already, is a line of sparkling water flavored with fruit juice, and their packaging is sexy AF. I was in Provincetown when I first saw it, waiting in line at Canteen for a late-night oyster roll. It was summer; boys in short-shorts and tank tops surrounded me. I couldn't have cared less—I only had eyes for a can of lemony sparkling water.

You know how this story typically goes: fall in lust with a product and take it home, only to have it disappoint. This happens to me all the time. But with Spindrift, things were different. The lemon-kissed bubbly tasted vibrant, alive, piquant, and refreshing. It both quenched my thirst and jolted me awake at the same time.

Spindrift's marketing line is that they're "America’s first and only line of sparkling waters made with real squeezed fruit." That's cool, I guess, but I'm less concerned with which drink came first and more concerned with what I want to drink when I'm blissed out on the inflatable pink swan my friends keep in their pool. Or what I'll want to pack in my survival kit (read: cooler) when I trek out to the beach. Or what I want to see when I open my fridge on those August days when it's too damn hot to cook, and in fact too damn hot to eat—too damn hot to do anything at all except drink bubbly water.

#### Instagram content

In the past that bubbly has been Vintage Seltzer—or, when I wanted a flavor, LaCroix. The Vintage cans will never go away (the bond between us is too strong), but given the choice between LaCroix and Spindrift, I'm currently choosing the latter.

Specifically I've fallen for that lemon flavor I saw in Provincetown. But I also like the blackberry, and—perhaps most of all—a brand new flavor, orange-mango, which is the sweetest Spindrift I've tried. (Don't get it twisted, it's just barely sweet—the hallmark of Spindrift is that it highlights a fruit's natural tartness. This, I think, it what makes the drinks so satisfying.) Also new as of today is a strawberry flavor, which is bizarre but not unpleasant—it focuses on strawberry's sour notes instead of the sweet ones.

But honestly, I won't be picky when it comes to Spindrift. They're all equally delicious, all equally as handsome. Give me the cucumber, or the grapefruit, or the raspberry-lime. I don't care, as long as I have a can in my hand. I can't help myself, honey. Spindrift has me thirsty.

BUY IT: Spindrift Seltzer Water, Lemon, 12 oz Cans (Pack of 4), $12.23 on Amazon

David joined Epicurious as its chief editor in 2015. His words and recipes have appeared in publications such as Bon Appetit, Healthyish, Gourmet, Cooking Light, Every Day with Rachael Ray, Food Network Magazine, The Guardian, Wine & Spirits, Time Out New York, and Time Out Chicago, where for many year

results.title: Why Spindrift Is My Favorite No-Sugar Seltzer
results.text:
Why Spindrift Is My Favorite No-Sugar Seltzer

Skip to Content

Gabby Romero

Delish editors handpick every product we feature. We may earn commission from the links on this page.

- I've tried dozens of seltzer brands, but my all-time favorite is Spindrift.
- The intense, all-natural flavor comes with no added sugar, so I love it way more than standard soda.
- My current favorite flavors are Half Tea & Half Lemon and Cranberry Punch.

---

I’m not really a soda girl, but you can almost always find me sipping sparkling water. I have my own water carbonator at home, I keep stashes of seltzer at all times, and I even have a desk-side mini fridge to keep extra cans within reach.

There’s a lot of nuance to fizzy water that varies based on where the water is sourced, how it’s carbonated, and what flavors are added. I’ve tried virtually every brand on the market to identify the best of the best. (If you want to know if your fave's on the list, check it out here.) But there’s one brand that keeps me coming back for more: Spindrift.

My first exposure to Spindrift was as a kid, when my mom brought a pack of grapefruit-flavored seltzer home from the grocery store. And I must admit, it didn’t make the best first impression. My sweet tooth was at its peak, and I couldn’t get behind the minimally sweet formula. But as an adult, I’ve made a complete 180.

Spindrift is the perfect drink—yeah, I said it. It’s effervescent without being overwhelming. The flavors are more intense than your average sparkling water, without any artificial flavors or added sugar. And the variety alone deserves a standing ovation.

There are currently 20 (!) varieties of Spindrift. I’ve tried most, if not all, of them and haven’t found a flop yet. Want something low-key? There are classic and refreshing options, such as Lemon and Grapefruit.

Want something a little more nuanced? Flavors like Cosnopolitan, Nojito, and Berry Belli-no are perfect as a standalone sip or an easy cocktail mixer. You really can’t go wrong.

My favorite flavor is constantly changing, but right now I’m reaching for the new seasonal Cranberry Punch flavor. It’s tart, tangy, and refreshing with the added zest of three types of citrus. And a perennial favorite is the Half Tea & Half Lemon flavor, which satisfies my constant Arnold Palmer cravings with zero grams of sugar.

And before you ask: no, Spindrift doesn’t sponsor me. (But if Team Spindrift wants to make me a brand ambassador, I wouldn’t turn it down.) I just wholeheartedly—and enthusiastically—recommend this brand. If you want something refreshing and all-natural that isn’t a sugary soda, it’s time to stock your fridge with Spindrift.

##### My Favorite Spindrift Flavors

###### Spindrift Cranberry Punch

###### Spindrift Island Punch

$19 at Amazon

results.title: Every Flavor of Spindrift, Tasted and Ranked | VinePair
results.author: Olivia White
results.text:
Every Flavor of Spindrift, Tasted and Ranked | VinePair

When it comes to sparkling water, we know a thing or two here at VinePair HQ. And in the sea of on-shelf options we’ve encountered, one brand continues to win over consumers coast to coast: Spindrift. Founded in 2010 by soda lover Bill Creelman, Spindrift almost immediately made a splash with its range made with real fruit juice. Today, the sparkling water brand is one of the most successful in the category, boasting over $300 million in sales per year, according to Bloomberg.

Given the demand for the bubbly beverage, it’s no surprise that Spindrift has expanded its lineup several times over its 14-year history. Now, there are 17 permanent expressions (and one seasonal flavor) under the brand’s umbrella, each with its own dedicated fan base. So, we decided to round up the line and get to work finding the best cans among them.

To streamline, we excluded the brand’s Half & Half and Mango Black Tea expressions and stuck with SKUs based solely with sparkling water. We were also forced to forgo tasting the Peach Strawberry flavor as we were unable to track down a can. When judging the 15 flavors we gathered, we considered overall flavor, carbonation level, sweetness, and “ahh” factor to solidify our rankings.

Curious to see where your favorite flavor of Spindrift ranks? Keep reading to discover our ranking of every Spindrift flavor, in order from “meh” to must-buy.

## 15. Pineapple

Don't Miss A Drop Get the latest in beer, wine, and cocktail culture sent straight to your inbox.

The Drop + VP Pro The Drop Only VP Pro Only

With real pineapple juice making up 8 percent of the can, there’s no missing the fruit’s influence here. It’s tropical and acidic with some pleasant underlying sweetness, but the beverage drinks more similarly to bubbled-up Dole than flavored water. While a great option for pineapple fanatics or those seeking a solid summer drink, we found the fruit’s flavor to be too dominant and somewhat cloying on the finish.

## 14. Grapeade

Spindrift’s Grapeade flavor is reminiscent of Welch’s grape juice thanks to the real Concord juice in every can, making it perfect for cracking open when childhood nostalgia strikes. That said, there’s a too-robust sweetness that lingers on the palate, and the weight of the juice smothers some of the carbonation. While it’s not the most refreshing of the bunch, Grapeade is sure to make you feel young again.

## 13. Mango Orange

This one is capital-J Juicy. Mango undeniably leads the charge here, providing sweetness and a punchy tropical fruit that dominates the palate. Orange juice adds a touch of acidity, though most of its citrus flavor gets overshadowed. This one is a little heavy and lacking in the carbonation department, but we’d still grab one in a pinch.

## 12. Island Punch

Made with passion fruit, orange, and guava sourced from all over the globe, Island Punch is one of the most dynamic expressions in Spindrift’s portfolio. The tropica

results.title: We Taste-Tested 19 Flavors of Spindrift—Here Are Our Favorites
results.text:
-- We Taste-Tested 19 Flavors of Spindrift—Here Are Our Favorites ​

Close

Serious Eats / Amanda Suarez

We tasted and ranked 19 flavors of Spindrift sparkling water and ranked them in order of our least to most favorite. Overall, we weren't big fans of the mocktail flavors, but several surprise hits had us dreaming of tropical vacations.

I cannot emphasize this enough: Our editors love sparkling water. Instead of huddling around a water cooler in the office, we huddle together in meetings, each gripping our respective cans of sparkling water. There are many sparkling water brands, but because some of us are particularly enthusiastic about Spindrift, we recently decided to try every single Spindrift flavor available to us.

We tasted them all, then ranked them in order from the one we'd keep on hand at all times to the one that would likely get pushed to the back of the fridge. Why did we do this? Because staying hydrated is essential, and this is clearly the most delicious way to do it—and also because we hope it'll help you narrow down the Spindrift options available at your local grocery store.

Because we were ranking these Spindrift flavors, we did not sample them blindly like we usually do for our taste tests. We did, however, sample them in random order, with each taster going in a different order to avoid palate fatigue skewing the results. After sipping our way through 19 different cans of sparkling water, we tabulated the results and came up with our definitive ranking. (With the exception of one or two of these, at least one person loved each one, and a lower rank doesn't mean it's not worth trying if you typically enjoy these flavor profiles.)

Serious Eats / Amanda Suarez

## 19. Nojito

We love a mojito, but this was a little too minty for our tasters. Nojito? No, thank you.

## 18. Cosnopolitan

Inspired by the Cosmopolitan cocktail, this Spindrift flavor gets its sweet-tart notes from cranberry juice, orange juice, and lime juice. "There's a slightly lemon-forward profile in this one," our associate culinary editor, Laila, observed. Though most of our editors wished there was more cranberry flavor, our senior social media editor, Kelli, enjoyed it just fine: "All that's missing is some booze, sugar, and lime."

## 17. Berry Belli-no

"This is the most flavorful and aromatic of the bunch," Laila noted. "It smells and tastes like peaches!" Made with both peach and strawberry purée, the Berry Belli-no smelled very much like a summer fruit cocktail. Though the beverage contains no added sugar, this flavor was still slightly too sweet for me, Laila, and our associate editorial director, Megan. Kelli and our associate visuals director, Amanda, however, were fans. "Look, I'm a child at heart and I do like the sugary one," Amanda wrote.

## 16. Grapeade

In the best way possible, this reminded me of grape soda and the Capri-Sun juice pouches I used to sip on as a child. "Lovely! Concord grape juice flavor, fresh and fizzy, tastes like f

results.title: The Best Spindrift Flavors, Ranked | PS Food
results.author: India Yaffe
results.text:
The Best Spindrift Flavors, Ranked | PS Food

Amazon Amazon

While each product featured is independently selected by our editors, we may include paid promotion. If you buy something through our links, we may earn commission. Read more about our Product Review Guidelines here.

I don't remember when I first had a Spindrift, but I do know that it quickly spiraled from a midday pick me up to a full blown obsession. I drink one every single day, and it's become something that I look forward to in the midst of busy workdays. It's the only sparkling water I've ever tried that actually uses real fruit to sweeten and flavor, which is something I really appreciate, and I'm not the only one; Kristen Bell loves it, too. Lots of other waters taste too strongly of chemicals and artificial flavors for my taste, but this is sweet, delicious, and healthy. To say I'm obsessed with Spindrift would be an understatement.

I'm so in love with Spindrift that I've converted not only my family but the rest of my POPSUGAR team as well. At my house, we buy in bulk; I'm talking 100 cans at a time because yes, it's that good. Now that you want to try Spindrift, you might be wondering where to start. Well, I've done the hard work for you and ranked my favorite flavors. There are 11 in total, and I've tried (almost) all of them. I would like to disclose that all opinions are my own, with absolutely no scientific testing to support my findings. Keep on reading, and then order some Spindrift for yourself.

12

amazon.com

## Cranberry Raspberry Spindrift Sparkling Water

Cranberry Raspberry Spindrift Sparkling Water($18 for 24) is the only slightly biased review you'll see here. I'm allergic to cranberry, so I've never actually tried this flavor. For this reason, it goes to the bottom.

Cranberry Raspberry Spindrift Sparkling WaterFrom$18$18 at amazon.com

11

amazon.com

## Half Tea & Half Lemon Spindrift Sparkling Water

Of all the flavors I've tried, the Half Tea & Half Lemon Spindrift Sparkling Water($18) is the only one that I felt did not live up to its hype. It tastes like a watery Arnold Palmer, which is one of my favorite drinks, and I don't think there's enough flavor here. Plus, the sparkling taste just felt a little off.

Half Tea & Half Lemon Spindrift Sparkling WaterFrom$18$18 at amazon.com

10

amazon.com

## Cucumber Spindrift Sparkling Water

The Cucumber Spindrift Sparkling Water($25 for 24) really does taste like spa water. It's refreshing, just not my personal favorite.

Cucumber Spindrift Sparkling WaterFrom$25$25 at amazon.com

## Pineapple Spindrift Sparkling Water

The Pineapple Spindrift Sparkling Water($26 for 24) is the brand's newest flavor, and the one I was the most excited for. It's delicious when mixed with a shot of vodka or tequila if you drink, but on its own, it felt a little sweet for me.

results.title: All 19 Spindrift Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Taste Test Guide
results.author: RON SILBERSTEIN
results.text:
All 19 Spindrift Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Taste Test Guide

Spindrift flavors ranked from worst to best – that is exactly what I set out to discover after noticing this sparkling water brand everywhere from grocery stores to office refrigerators. Unlike typical sparkling waters that use natural flavors (a vague term that often means artificial), Spindrift stands apart by using real fruit juice and fruit purees in every can.

Our team spent three weeks testing 19 different Spindrift varieties. We tasted them chilled, at room temperature, and even as cocktail mixers. Some flavors blew us away with their authenticity. Others missed the mark entirely. I will walk you through every single flavor, explain why each ranks where it does, and help you avoid wasting money on the duds.

By the end of this guide, you will know exactly which Spindrift flavors deserve space in your refrigerator and which ones you should leave on the shelf.

Table of Contents

Toggle

## Our Taste Testing Methodology

Before diving into the rankings, let me explain how we evaluated each flavor. All testing occurred in our office kitchen over three weeks in March 2026. We purchased every flavor available at three major retailers to ensure we did not miss any varieties.

Each flavor underwent testing at two temperatures: straight from the refrigerator at 38 degrees Fahrenheit, and after sitting out for 30 minutes to simulate a beach or poolside scenario. We evaluated five criteria for every can: flavor accuracy (does it taste like the actual fruit), sweetness level, carbonation intensity, refreshment factor, and overall drinkability. Multiple team members scored each flavor independently, and we averaged the results to create these rankings.

One important note: Spindrift contains real juice, which means the flavors taste different from artificially-flavored sparkling waters like LaCroix. Some people love this intensity. Others find it overwhelming. Keep your personal preferences in mind as you read these rankings.

## The Bottom Tier: Flavors to Skip

Let us start with the flavors that disappointed us. These ranked at the bottom of our taste test for various reasons – some tasted artificial despite the real juice claim, others had strange aftertastes, and a few simply lacked the refreshment factor we want from sparkling water.

### 19. Nojito

Nojito attempts to capture the essence of a mojito without the alcohol. The concept sounds appealing, but the execution falls flat. The mint flavor dominates aggressively, overwhelming any lime notes present. Several testers described the aftertaste as reminiscent of chewing gum left in a hot car.

The real problem with Nojito is the imbalance. Real mojitos achieve harmony between mint, lime, and sweetness. This canned version tips too far toward the herbal mint side, creating something that feels more like a mouthwash than a refreshing beverage. Skip this one unless you absolutely love intense mint flavors.

### 18. Cosmopolitan

The Co

results.title: Every Spindrift Flavor Ranked From Worst To Best
results.text:
Every Spindrift Flavor Ranked From Worst To Best

# Every Spindrift Flavor Ranked From Worst To Best

By Jennifer Goldsmith May 11, 2022 2:46 pm EST

Sheila Fitzgerald/Shutterstock

Spindrift Sparkling Water is known for its fresh-squeezed fruit that is sourced from farmers across the United States, giving it a refreshing and pulpy flavor profile that sets it apart from other sparkling waters on the market. From the Pacific Northwest to sunny Florida to Spindrift's home state of Massachusetts, real fruit is picked fresh from the source to create every unique flavor of Spindrift. Spindrift was created and founded by Bill Creelman — a small-town, Massachusetts-born entrepreneur who dreamt of creating a refreshing artisan beverage that could be accessible to all (and who doesn't like to support other's dreams coming true?). According to Thrillist, the word "Spindrift" refers to the whitewash of a wave break, lending the brand an element of cool that reverberates loudly among sparkling water fans. Plus, every can of Spindrift Sparkling Water is recyclable and you're drinking real fruit instead of "natural flavors" like other sparkling water brands on the market tout.

Spindrift also holds values in terms of humans and wildlife, and to date, Spindrift has donated $25K to the NAACP, matched employee donations to Black Lives Matter, and they give back to Australia's Wildlife Information Rescue and Education Service that helps animals in the aftermath of life-threatening bushfires.

While there really is no bad option when it comes to drinking Spindrift, there are certainly a lot of choices, so we surveyed some experts in the field of sparkling water to gather our list of worst to best flavors of Spindrift.

## 12. Blood Orange Tangerine

Twitter

The newest creation by Spindrift sparkling water founders and farmers, Blood Orange Tangerine is so fresh on the market that the verdict is still out on how well it will perform, having launched it in the spring of 2022. That being said, with no other blood orange tangerine flavor of sparkling water on the market to compete with, Spindrift creators are certain this one will be a big hit within and outside of their community of avid followers — or "Drifters" — as they are referred to on the inside.

The union of citrusy, almost bitter blood oranges combined with the sweetness of ripe tangerine creates an "avantbgarde" sparkling water sensation unlike any other, said a Spindrift innovator on the team's webpage announcing the new varietal. It's also worth mentioning that Spindrift's new Blood Orange Tangerine contains 2 grams of sugar, 3 grams of carbohydrates and 12 calories per serving, according to Food Business News.

## 11. Cranberry Raspberry

Twitter

A unique combination in the sparkling water department, Spindrift's Cranberry Raspberry Sparkling Water is the result of an East Coast meets West Coast fruit concoction, with cranberries sourced from local cranberry bogs in Massachusetts in the fall, which are
